title: Hardware, Binary System & Data Units – Page 490
section: Computer: Hardware, Binary System, Data Measurement & Logic Gates

questions.question: A computer has two main parts:
questions.explanation: Page 490: It has two main parts: (i) Hardware and (ii) Software.

questions.question: Hardware comprises of the physical units of a computer system. These units constitute the:
questions.explanation: Page 490: A computer has three main units: Input unit, Processing unit, Output unit. These constitute the hardware.

questions.question: A computer has three main units:

options.B: Input unit, Processing unit, Output unit

questions.explanation: Page 490: A computer has three main units: 1. Input unit, 2. Processing unit and 3. Output unit.

questions.question: The computer has its own internal:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The computer has its own internal 'language'.

questions.question: The computer is essentially made of electronic components that can generate either a low (0 volt) or a high (say 5 volts). These states are represented by:
questions.explanation: Page 490: A high is represented by '1' and a low by '0'. The 1s and 0s are known as binary digits or 'bits'.

questions.question: Computers always work with:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Computers always work with bits. They do not understand any other form.

questions.question: Computer scientists use combination of how many bits to represent various symbols?
questions.explanation: Page 490: Computer scientists use combination of 8 bits taken together to represent various symbols.

questions.question: Using 8 bits, the computer can represent how many different symbols?
questions.explanation: Page 490: Because every bit can take one of 2 possible values, using 8 bits, the computer can represent 256 different symbols.

questions.question: A combination of 8 bits is called a:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Such a combination of 8 bits is called a byte.

questions.question: In computers, information is represented using multiples of 8 (2³) bits. Higher units are expressible in multiples of:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Since 8 bits are the smallest unit of information, higher units are expressible in multiples of 2^3.

questions.question: The power of two closest to a kilo (1000) is 2^10 =:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The power of two closest to a kilo (1000) is 2^10 = 1024.

questions.question: 1 Mega Byte is equal to:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Mega Bytes is used in conventional sense equal to 1 million bytes or 1000 Kilo Bytes.

questions.question: A Giga Byte refers to approximately:
questions.explanation: Page 490: A Giga Byte refers to 1000 megabytes approximately or 1 million kilobytes.

questions.question: 4 Bit = :
questions.explanation: Page 490: 4 Bit = 1 Nibble.

questions.question: 2 Nibble (8 Bit) = :
questions.explanation: Page 490: 2 Nibble (8 Bit) = 1 Byte.

questions.question: 1024 Byte = :
questions.explanation: Page 490: 1024 Byte = 1 Kilo Byte (KB).

questions.question: 1024 KB = :
questions.explanation: Page 490: 1024 KB = 1 Mega Byte (MB).

questions.question: 1024 MB = :
questions.explanation: Page 490: 1024 MB = 1 Giga Byte (GB).

questions.question: 1024 GB = :
questions.explanation: Page 490: 1024 GB = 1 Tera Byte (TB).

questions.question: 1024 TB = :
questions.explanation: Page 490: 1024 TB = 1 Peta Byte (PB).

questions.question: The basic elements of computers that can signal a 1 or 0 are called:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The basic elements that can signal a 1 or 0 are called flip-flops. It is a simple electrical device.

questions.question: Modern computers use a very tiny set of flip-flops known as:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Modern computers use a very tiny set of flip-flops known as a register.

questions.question: The entire independent circuits can be designed on a small piece of semi conductor material like:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The entire independent circuits can be designed on a small piece of semi conductor material like silicon.

questions.question: Silicon is obtained from sand and is a poor conductor of electricity. Miniature circuits made on silicon are called:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Silicon from sand is a poor conductor. Such miniature circuits are called Integrated Circuits (IC).

questions.question: By 1971, engineers were able to build a complete computer on a single chip of silicon called:
questions.explanation: Page 490: By 1971, engineers built a complete computer on a single silicon chip called the microprocessor.

questions.question: The computer converts all decimal numbers into:

options.B: Binary numbers or combinations of bits

questions.explanation: Page 490: The computer converts all decimal numbers into binary numbers or combinations of bits.

questions.question: The internal circuits that perform mathematical operations on bits are usually made of:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The internal circuits are usually made of two or more logic gates. Logic gates generate a 1 or 0 depending on input.

questions.question: The three basic logic gates are:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The three basic logic gates are AND, OR and NOT.

questions.question: A computer is organized into three basic units:
questions.explanation: Page 490: A computer is organized into three basic units: 1. CPU, 2. Memory Unit (MU), 3. Input/Output Unit.

questions.question: The CPU is the part of a computer that performs the main function of:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The CPU is the part that performs the main function of information processing.

questions.question: While earliest machines were 8-bit, contemporary computers can work with:
questions.explanation: Page 490: While earliest machines were 8-bit, contemporary computers can work with 16, 32 and even 64 bits. This is called data width.

questions.question: The earliest computers were designed so that their components could work with how many bits at a time?
questions.explanation: Page 490: The earliest computers were designed so that there components could work with 8 bits at a time.

questions.question: Hertz is a unit of:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Hertz is a unit of frequency.

questions.question: 1 MHz equals:
questions.explanation: Page 490: 1 MHz = 1 million 'ticks' per second.

questions.question: 1 GHz = :
questions.explanation: Page 490: 1 GHz = 1000 MHz.

questions.question: The ticks per second of the internal clock are measured in:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The ticks per second of the internal clock are measured in megahertz and gigahertz.

questions.question: Higher the clock-speed of CPU:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Higher the clock-speed, faster the computer.

questions.question: A flip-flop is a simple:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Flip-flop is a simple electrical device and can either be a '0' or a '1', meaning current flow is either inward or outward.

questions.question: The most important characteristic of registers is that binary digits can be stored in them using:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The most important characteristic of registers is that binary digits can be stored in them using certain voltages.

questions.question: The binary number system is similar to decimal system. The only difference is in:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The binary number system is similar to decimal. Only difference is in the number of digits used (2 vs 10).

questions.question: In binary system, only which digits are used?
questions.explanation: Page 490: Binary uses only 0 and 1, while decimal uses 0-9.

questions.question: 8 bits are enough to cover our entire range of alphabets, numbers and special characters like $, @, +, ₹. This is because 2^8 =:
questions.explanation: Page 490: 8 bits = 256 combinations, enough for all alphabets, numbers and special characters.

questions.question: To build complex information like paragraphs, we need larger numbers of bytes called:
questions.explanation: Page 490: For complex information we need larger number of bytes - kilobyte, megabyte and gigabyte.

questions.question: Logic gates are components that generate a 1 or a 0 depending on:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Logic gates are components that generate a 1 or a 0 depending on the input.

questions.question: Computer scientists use a special convention: A high is represented by '1' and a low is represented by:
questions.explanation: Page 490: A high is symbolically represented by a '1' and a low is represented by a '0'.

questions.question: It is difficult to talk always in terms of currents and voltages. Therefore computer scientists represent information using:
questions.explanation: Page 490: Computer scientists use binary convention: high=1, low=0.

questions.question: The term 'binary' refers to something that has:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The term 'binary' refers to something that has two parts.

questions.question: Linus released Linux under:

options.B: General Public Licence (open and free to all)

questions.explanation: Page 490: Linus released Linux under General Public Licence, making it open and free to all.

questions.question: A computer may be used to control purely mechanical action. It has:

options.B: Two main parts: Hardware and Software

questions.explanation: Page 490: A computer may be used to control purely mechanical action. It has two main parts: Hardware and Software.

questions.question: The computer supplies processed information back to the users using special:
questions.explanation: Page 490: The computer supplies processed information back to the users using special output devices.
